mercury.description: The smallest planet and closest to the Sun. It has no atmosphere.
funFacts: One day on Mercury lasts 59 Earth days
Mercury has the most eccentric orbit in the Solar System

venus.description: The hottest planet in the Solar System with a thick CO₂ atmosphere.
funFacts: Venus rotates backwards compared to most planets
A day on Venus is longer than its year

earth.description: Our planet. 71% of the surface is covered by water and it's the only known planet that supports life.
funFacts: Earth's core is as hot as the surface of the Sun
Earth rotates at 1,670 km/h at the equator

mars.description: The Red Planet. It has the tallest mountain and deepest valley in the Solar System.
funFacts: Mars has seasons like Earth
Olympus Mons is 3x taller than Mount Everest

jupiter.description: The largest planet in the Solar System. A gas giant with mass 2x the total of all other planets.
funFacts: The Great Red Spot is a storm lasting over 350 years
Jupiter's magnetic field is 20,000x stronger than Earth's

saturn.description: The planet with beautiful rings. A gas giant less dense than water.
funFacts: Saturn's rings are made of ice and rock
Saturn could float in a large enough bathtub

uranus.description: An ice giant that rotates on its side (98 degrees tilt).
funFacts: Uranus rotates tilted at 98 degrees on its side
Uranus was the first planet discovered with a telescope

neptune.description: The windiest planet in the Solar System. Supersonic winds reach 2,100 km/h.
funFacts: Neptune has the fastest winds in the Solar System
Neptune takes 165 years to orbit the Sun

sun.description: The star at the center of our Solar System. It contains 99.86% of the total mass.
funFacts: The Sun is 4.6 billion years old
Light from the Sun takes 8 minutes to reach Earth

moon.description: Earth's only natural satellite. It influences tides and stabilizes Earth's axial tilt.
funFacts: The Moon is slowly drifting away from Earth at 3.8 cm per year
The same side of the Moon always faces Earth
